verse_text: So the people served the Lord all the days of Joshua, and all the days of the elders who outlived Joshua, who saw every great deed the Lord did for Israel.

links.context: , 2 Once Joshua died (recorded both in [[JOS.24:30]]-32 and [[JDG.2:7]]-9), the question became, "Who will lead Israel in the fight against the Canaanites?" This represents the transition from the people under Joshua generally obeying God and experiencing victory to their lapsing into idolatry and disobedience under the judges. Judah is a reference to the tribe.

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
